description: Add pagination to GET /users. The endpoint should accept optional query parameters ?page= (1-based, default 1) and ?limit= (default 20, max 100). The response should return { data: [...], total: N, page: N, limit: N }.
expected_behavior: GET /users?page=2&limit=1 returns the second user with pagination metadata. GET /users (no params) still works with defaults.

success_criteria.description: Implementation reads page and limit from req.query
Response includes total count alongside data
Implementation slices the array correctly using page and limit
Page and limit have sensible defaults when not provided
success_criteria.rubric: Does the implementation provide default values for page (1) and limit (20) when the query parameters are absent? Are invalid values (e.g. page=0, limit=200) handled gracefully?
